title: Pocket Hole Planters
summary: A simple square planter box on 2x2 legs, built entirely with pocket holes and a slatted bottom, sized shorter or taller with the same basic construction.
description:
This is a flexible pattern more than a single fixed size — build the sides taller or shorter than shown and the construction stays exactly the same, since every joint is a pocket hole and there's no interlocking joinery to redesign around a new height. Build the two end panels first, then add the leg cleats while the ends are still flat and easy to clamp, since reaching into an assembled box to attach cleats is much harder than doing it before the sides go on.

Line the inside with hardware cloth, tar paper, or a plastic liner before adding soil — the slatted bottom is there for drainage, and without a liner, soil (and the moisture in it) will work its way straight through the gaps and rot the slats from the inside out.

materials.name: Pine, 1x6, 8 ft
materials.note: 3 - 1x4, 8 ft can be substituted for the sides instead.

materials.name: Hardware cloth, tar paper, or plastic liner
materials.note: Keeps soil from working through the slatted bottom.

cutList.note: Or 16 pieces of 1x4 at the same length if substituting.

steps.title: Build the two ends
steps.body: Build two identical end panels from the side boards, joined with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Sides can be built shorter or taller than shown — the construction is the same regardless of height.

steps.title: Add the leg cleats
steps.body: Attach the 2x2 legs to the two end panels while they're still flat — this is easier to do now than after the sides are on.

steps.title: Add the sides
steps.body: Attach the remaining side boards to join the two ends into a box, using pocket holes and screws.

steps.title: Lay the bottom slats and finish
steps.body: Lay the 1x2 slats across the bottom for drainage. Line the inside with hardware cloth, tar paper, or a plastic liner before adding soil to protect the wood. Add optional craft finials to the tops of the legs. Sand and finish.
materials: Hardware cloth, tar paper, or plastic liner

images.alt: A square wood planter box on 2x2 legs with a slatted bottom
